source.name: SSA Canonical Catalog
notes: Classic Levantine herb salad: parsley-forward with mint, tomato, lemon, and bulgur.
Designed as a baseline for planners (veg-forward side; acid + herb signature).

yield.notes: Makes ~4–5 cups depending on herb volume and tomato juiciness.

ingredients.label: Fine bulgur wheat
ingredients.notes: Fine bulgur (No. 1) is traditional. If using medium bulgur, allow extra soak time or briefly simmer.

ingredients.label: Boiling water
ingredients.notes: For soaking bulgur (or use very hot water).

ingredients.label: Flat-leaf parsley, finely chopped
ingredients.notes: About 2 large bunches. Must be very dry after washing to avoid watery salad.

ingredients.label: Fresh mint, finely chopped
ingredients.notes: Optional but classic. Adjust to taste.

ingredients.label: Tomatoes, seeded and finely diced
ingredients.notes: Seeding reduces excess juice (keeps tabbouleh bright, not soggy).

ingredients.label: Green onions (scallions), finely sliced
ingredients.notes: About 4–5 scallions. Can substitute 30 g finely diced red onion.

ingredients.label: Fresh lemon juice
ingredients.notes: Start here; tabbouleh should be lemon-bright.

ingredients.label: Fine salt
ingredients.notes: Add gradually; tomatoes vary in salt needs.

steps.title: Soak the bulgur
steps.body: Place bulgur in a bowl. Pour boiling water over it, stir once, cover, and let stand until tender and fluffy. When ready, drain any excess water through a fine mesh strainer and press gently to remove moisture.

steps.title: Wash, dry, and chop herbs very finely
steps.body: Wash parsley and mint. Dry extremely well (salad spinner + towels). Remove tough parsley stems. Chop herbs finely—tabbouleh should be delicate, not chunky.

steps.title: Dice tomatoes and slice onions
steps.body: Seed tomatoes (remove watery core) and dice very small. Slice scallions thinly. If using red onion, dice finely and rinse briefly under cold water to mellow bite (optional).

steps.title: Combine bulgur, herbs, and vegetables
steps.body: In a large bowl, combine soaked bulgur, parsley, mint, tomatoes, and scallions. Toss to distribute evenly.

steps.title: Dress with lemon and olive oil; balance salt and acid
steps.body: Add lemon juice, olive oil, salt, and pepper (optional). Toss well. Taste and adjust: tabbouleh should be bright and lemon-forward with enough salt to lift the herbs.

steps.title: Rest briefly and re-taste
steps.body: Let stand 10 minutes for flavors to meld, then re-taste and adjust lemon/salt as needed. Serve as part of a mezze plate or alongside grilled meats/fish.

refrigerate.notes: Best within 24 hours. Herbs soften over time; refresh with lemon and a drizzle of olive oil before serving.

variations.title: Gluten-free tabbouleh (quinoa)
changes: Replace bulgur with 120 g cooked and cooled quinoa.
Keep lemon-forward dressing; reduce olive oil slightly if quinoa feels oily.

variations.title: Herb salad (no bulgur)
changes: Omit bulgur entirely and increase tomatoes to 320 g and scallions to 70 g.
This becomes a parsley-mint salad similar to some modern tabbouleh styles.

variations.title: Add cucumber (crunchy)
changes: Add 150 g finely diced cucumber (seeded) for extra crunch.
Salt lightly and drain cucumber 5 minutes to reduce wateriness.

notes: Key success factor: DRY herbs and SEEDED tomatoes to avoid watery tabbouleh.
Flavor target: lemon-bright, herb-forward, lightly rich from olive oil.
If making ahead, store dressing separate and combine shortly before serving for best texture.
